_comment: ApiGate config — drop into the root of any repo as .apigate.config.json. All fields optional; defaults shown below.
_frameworks: Toggle individual parsers. Useful for muting Fastify on Express-only repos to keep noise out of the report.
_auth: Identifier names that mean 'this endpoint is guarded' when present in the middleware/decorator chain. Add your project's custom auth helpers here. ApiGate cannot inspect what these identifiers DO at runtime — only their declared presence.
_failOn: Exit-code policy. openWriteMethods=true means an unauthenticated POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE causes exit 1 (recommended). openReadMethods=true also fails on unauthenticated GET (strict). unknown=true fails when ApiGate cannot prove a discovered endpoint's posture. drift=true also fails on any spec-drift item. intentionalPublic=true makes the exit-1 gate ignore the public-auth heuristic — i.e. EVERY open endpoint fails (very strict; for compliance audits). newOpenWrite=true (default ON, only meaningful when --diff is provided) fails when the PR adds any new OPEN write endpoint.
_publicAuthPatterns: Override the built-in public-auth heuristic. null (default) uses DEFAULT_PUBLIC_AUTH_PATTERNS from lib/heuristics.mjs — common login/signup/refresh/health paths. Provide an array of { method, path } pairs to replace entirely, or an empty array [] to disable the heuristic. The exact-match list is deterministic: paths are normalized (`/users/:id` ↔ `/users/{id}` ↔ `/users/<id>`) before comparison.
_requireSpec: When true, exit 1 if no OpenAPI 2/3 spec is detected. Useful when spec drift is part of the contract and a green no-spec run would be misleading.
_strictPublic: When true, disable the built-in public-auth heuristic unless publicAuthPatterns is explicitly provided. Useful for repos that want every public endpoint declared locally.
_excludePaths: Glob list of files whose endpoints are excluded from the report. Applied AFTER parsing — does not speed up scanning.
_severityOverrides: Pin specific (method, path) pairs to a risk tier. Applied AFTER the default risk ladder. Example: [{ "method": "POST", "path": "/admin/wipe", "risk": "LOW", "reason": "scheduled maintenance only" }]. Match is exact on (method, path) — the path is matched against the resolved/normalized form ApiGate prints in the report.
